name: Dagster
what_it_does: Asset-based data orchestration platform that models pipelines as a graph of data assets with dependencies, offering a GraphQL API for pipeline management and observability in data platform engineering.
use_cases: Triggering asset materialization runs programmatically for downstream pipeline automation
Querying asset lineage and materialization history for data observability workflows
Managing run schedules, sensors, and backfills via API
Building data platform dashboards from Dagster's rich pipeline metadata
Integrating data asset status into broader orchestration agents
not_for: Real-time stream processing (batch-oriented; use Kafka or Flink)
Simple scripts without asset dependency modeling needs
Organizations preferring simpler task-based orchestration over asset-centric model
best_when: You're building a data platform with complex asset dependencies and want software engineering best practices (typing, testing, lineage) applied to data pipelines.
avoid_when: Your data pipelines are simple, your team prefers task-based (not asset-based) thinking, or you already have Airflow or Prefect deeply integrated.

auth.notes: Dagster Cloud uses user tokens and agent tokens. Self-hosted Dagster has no auth by default. Cloud tokens are user-scoped without fine-grained per-asset permissions.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Open source self-hosted: free; Dagster Cloud Serverless: free for 1 user with limits
pricing.notes: Open source version is completely free and fully functional. Cloud adds managed infrastructure and team features.

performance.rate_limits: No documented rate limits for self-hosted; Cloud has undisclosed limits

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: GraphQL API errors returned in errors array. Run failure details accessible via run logs API. Error context is good but requires GraphQL knowledge.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Asset materialization requests create new runs — not idempotent. Asset definitions themselves are idempotent on redeploy.
known_agent_gotchas: API is GraphQL only — no REST alternative — agents must construct GraphQL queries
Asset keys are hierarchical paths (e.g., ['schema', 'table']) — agents must use correct key format
The asset-centric model is a paradigm shift from task-based orchestration — query patterns differ significantly
Self-hosted Dagster has no authentication by default — add auth middleware before exposing to agents
Run tags and metadata are the primary way to correlate Dagster runs with external systems — use them consistently
